title: Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on adopting Neste Operations Excellence Management System (OEMS) and translate it to Singapore refinery Integrated Management System (IMS) with ISO 9001, 14001, 45001, SS651 and best practices. You will provide your expertise to lead our Singapore Refinery IMS, ensuring safety compliance are analysed, updated and sustained. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the Singapore Safety department. Your key stakeholders will range from engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore to the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. As the local administrator of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System, you are expected to manage, analyse, trend, gain insights and learnings from Refinery unsafe conditions, near misses, incidents and audit management. In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager and support the safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Audits Lead & coordinate audits, such as ISO certification, recertifications, internal/external audits, regulatory audit (eg. SS651), Corporate Insurance Survey and Neste OEMS (Operations Excellence Management System) Principle / compliance audit and self assessment. Ensure audits action closure, preparation and follow up are sustainable. IMS (Integrated Management System -  Neste OEMS, SS651, ISO 9001, 14001, 45001) Lead, develop and implement Singapore refinery IMS Prepare and facilitate annual Management Review, support the Management Representative, follow up annual Management Review recommendations and track area of improvement closure Be the NMS (Neste Management System) Space-owner and liaise with the cross functional team on QMS  activities (i.e.NMS Space/procedure/Audits) Gatekeeping/Maintaining NMS documentation framework to ensure compliances are met throughout the certification period. Compliance/Legal Register Review quarterly Legal Register related to Health, Safety, Environmental legal requirements & ISO Standards Work with relevant stakeholders to interpret legal requirements Review compliance activities on a monthly basis Owner of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System Own Singapore refinery NCR System, processing of NCR reportings, programs owner and be its user administrator Lead & drive Near Miss reporting program such as data trending, analysis, proposals and  recommendations for corrective and preventive actions Incident Learning Own and manage the Incident Learning processes (i.e. reporting, classification, investigation & actions) to ensure compliance with corporate, regulatory & site requirements Advise stakeholders on incident classification and reportable incidents Participate in incident investigations as Safety SME or as team leader Lead cross functional team to trend and propose improvement plans to reduce incident rate and improve safety culture Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 8 years safety working experience in petrochemical or chemical industry Bachelor’s Degree in chemical / process / process safety engineering; having a Masters Degree in a related field is highly desired Strong understanding and knowledge to lead, support, implement and operationalise IMS to the workforce Able to effectively lead organization in audit preparations, execution, follow up closure and analysis of long term effectiveness, durability and on-going compliance of implemented actions Ability to interpret corporate and regulatory requirements and effectively translate them into practical, organization-specific solutions while ensuring stakeholder alignment Experienced incident investigation leader or facilitator Strong communication, writing and analytical skill Registered WSHO is an added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request). We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Assistant Manager, Business Intelligence
description: The Assistant Manager supports the Head of Business Intelligence in driving data‑driven decision-making and enhancing operational performance. This role combines BI expertise with operational management capabilities, ensuring that both data functions and customer service functions run efficiently, accurately, and in alignment with organisational goals. Key Responsibilities Lead the collection, cleansing, validation, and analysis of operational and strategic datasets to support reporting, forecasting, and performance optimisation. Oversee the development, enhancement, and governance of dashboards, reports, and visualisations using tools such as Power BI. Conduct deep-dive analysis to uncover trends, inefficiencies, and opportunities for improvement, and present insights to senior stakeholders. Ensure data integrity, accuracy, and security across all BI processes and outputs. Manage and improve data pipelines, data collection processes, and integration with internal and external systems (e.g., automation, cloud platforms). Provide mentorship, supervision, and technical guidance to BI executives and analysts. Analyse call centre performance data and recommend process improvements. Collaborate closely with operational teams to address customer issues, optimise workflows, and enhance customer experience. Develop SOPs, scripts, and knowledge-base materials to support frontline teams. Coordinate with IT and system vendors for system enhancements, troubleshooting, and workflow automation. Support the planning and execution of cross-functional projects related to data strategy, digital transformation, process improvement, and customer service. Prepare management reports, operational reviews, and insights decks for senior leadership. Perform any other related duties or initiatives as assigned. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Analytics, Statistics, Operations Management, or a related field. 5–7 years of experience in business intelligence, data analytics, or related functions. At least 2 years of supervisory or team lead experience, preferably managing both data teams and operations teams. Strong proficiency in data visualisation tools such as Power BI (preferred) or Tableau. Solid understanding of data models, ETL processes, and data warehousing concepts. Experience working with cloud platforms such as Azure or AWS is an advantage. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills. Demonstrated ability to manage cross-functional projects and drive continuous improvement. Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate data insights into actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.

title: Sales Engineer
description: About the Position We are seeking an energetic and customer-focused Sales Engineer to drive sales of the company’s products, integrated solutions, and services. In this role, you will support the Business Development team in preparing competitive proposals and tender submissions, gathering technical and costing information, and delivering pre-sales solutions tailored to client requirements. Key Responsibilities Promote and sell the company's products, integrated solutions, and services. Work closely with the Business Development (BD) Manager in the preparation of tender bid submissions and proposals, including gathering functional and technical information and costing from in-house departments and/or from other vendors and OEM partners. Responsible for pre-sales solution and proposal submission preparation for the assigned Request for Quote (RFQ) / Request for Proposal (RFP) from our clients, and promptly update the status to the relevant BD Manager Participate in sales presentations, maintain technical application and presentation materials (such as digital campaigns and marketing collateral, including brochures, application notes, the company’s website, and LinkedIn). Be involved and assist in the business division to explore new solutions/products to support the company’s business goals and to identify new business opportunities and expand our market share. Requirement Diploma or Degree in Electrical/ Electronics/ Computer, equivalent Engineering discipline. Relevant experiences in any of these SCADA/PLC solutions, Building Management System, relevant Operation Technologies (OT/IOT) solution for Power, Water, Transportation industries are preferred. Candidate with knowledge in OT network & cybersecurity solutions / Security & Surveillance solutions will have an advantage. Strong communication and presentation skills. Adaptable and self-driven to acquire new technologies. Able to work independently and be goal-oriented for assigned tasks. Able to exhibit good qualities of an effective team player when working within a team.

title: Risk Advisory Associate (Internal Audit)
description: Kickstart Your Career in Risk Advisory If you're a fresh graduate or early-career professional eager to build a meaningful career in risk advisory, this is your chance to be part of a dynamic and growing team that delivers impactful insights to both private and public sector clients. We are looking for motivated, curious and analytical individuals to join us as Associates in our Risk Advisory team . This is an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience across a range of projects and industries, with guidance and mentorship from experienced professionals. At CLA Global TS, we also believe that meaningful work is best done in a positive and connected environment. From casual team lunches and festive celebrations to learning trips and wellness activities, you’ll be part of a team that values both collaboration and camaraderie! What You’ll Do: As an Associate, you’ll play an active role in our projects from day one: Participate in internal control reviews, risk assessments, and compliance checks across various industries Conduct walkthroughs and tests of business processes to assess control effectiveness Assist in data analysis to uncover patterns, gaps or inconsistencies Support the preparation of client-ready reports and working papers Join meetings with clients to observe and contribute to risk discussions Learn to think critically, ask the right questions, and develop a strong sense of professional skepticism You’ll also be exposed to diverse projects, both local and international , including: Internal Control Reviews Enterprise risk management Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) compliance reviews Operational audits IPO readiness and compliance assessments Who We’re Looking For: We’re not just looking for experience, we’re looking for potential : Degree in Accountancy, Business, Risk Management, Economics, Data Analytics, Information Systems or  Political Science Candidates with 1–2 years’ experience will be considered for the Associate role, while candidates with 3 or more years’ experience may be considered for the Senior position. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills Eager to learn and open to new challenges Able to work well independently and in teams Proficient in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) Detail-oriented with a strong sense of curiosity Willingness to travel overseas

title: Senior Product Manager (Health Technology)
description: About Us Mesh Bio is a leading digital health company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through advanced analytics solutions. Mesh Bio has received international recognition and accolades. Our platform leverages predictive models to improve patient outcomes, helping healthcare providers offer more personalized, data-driven care. We are looking for an experienced professional to lead the deployment of our cutting-edge software solutions to healthcare providers. The Senior Product Manager (“Manager”) is a key commercial position that is dedicated to delivering Mesh Bio’s healthcare software solutions to customers and partners. The Manager is expected to work together with other company functions, including the Engineering and the Products team in meeting customers’ needs. Responsibilities and Duties Explore and evaluate new opportunities, validate customers, identify new business models and areas where Mesh Bio’s solutions can add significant, differentiated value. Lead the customer’s discovery process for new products, helping to unearth customer’s business objectives and current pain points and translate to user requirements specification. Draft up product requirements specifications which matches product features and capability built on users’ requirements. Act as a key liaison between Products, Sales, and Engineering, funnelling customer feedback to help shape the product roadmap. Assist in conducting competitive analyses and preparing go-to-market plans for new products, including early-stage needs finding, user research, market analysis. Work closely with the Products and the Engineering teams to build prototypes, test, and iterate. Conduct Proof-of-Concepts/Pilots where necessary to help customers validate technical requirements. Assist in delivering value-based demonstrations highlighting our platform’s ecosystem Lead and manage the implementation of product new features, along with the Engineering teams. Serves as Technical Lead for Healthcare Solutions Engineers, Customer Success Specialists and managing their tech related issues and communicate with Engineers. Assist in managing the implementation and onboarding of new customers Own and drive the product vision, roadmap strategy, ensuring alignment with business goals and customer needs. Serves as a key liaison within the commercial teams in meeting the needs of ISO27001 and ISO13485 Contribute to the success of a rapidly changing and growing organization Any ad hoc work related tasks as mutually agreed with direct superior. QUALIFICATIONS: Min Bachelor’s degree in Life Sciences, Nursing, Biomedical engineering or equivalent 3 - 5 years prior experience working with an early-stage proprietary product in a life science, healthcare, or medical device setting Comfortable and confident speaking to doctors and healthcare workers Comfortable with collaboration, open communication and reaching across functional borders Ability to multi-task and manage tasks with varying priorities Personality requirements: Self-starter, client-oriented, independent, and able to function in a dynamic start-up environment. What We Offer - Opportunity to work on cutting-edge digital health solutions with a global impact. - A dynamic and collaborative work environment. - Competitive compensation and benefits package. - Career growth and professional development opportunities. How to Apply If you are excited about shaping the future of digital health and have the skills and experience we are looking for, we would love to hear from you! Please send your resume and a cover letter outlining your qualifications and motivation to careers@meshbio.com . Mesh Bio Pte Ltd is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to fostering an inclusive workforce. We do not discriminate based on race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We believe in attracting, developing, and retaining a diverse team of talented individuals. Our hiring decisions are based on qualifications, merit, and business need. We encourage individuals from all backgrounds to apply for opportunities with us. Join us in our mission to create an inclusive and welcoming environment where everyone can thrive and contribute their unique perspectives and talents.

title: Research Fellow (Operations Research & Analytics)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Role Summary: Lead and manage multi-stakeholder research projects in operations research, analytics, and optimization, including collaborations with NTUC FairPrice (Plug-in Flow Routing Optimization Engine; Store Friendly replenishment packing) and PSA (data analysis to improve efficiency in the container logistics ecosystem), as well as other industry and academic projects. Drive end-to-end project delivery—from problem framing and model formulation to implementation, evaluation, and dissemination—while coordinating milestones and deliverables with collaborators. Key Responsibilities: Act as project manager for assigned projects, developing work plans, timelines, risk registers, and progress reports; ensure on-time delivery of milestones and industry-facing deliverables. Work closely with external collaborators (e.g., NTUC FairPrice, Store Friendly, PSA) and internal stakeholders to define objectives, align expectations, and translate operational needs into rigorous research questions. Design and formulate models for routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization; develop algorithms and decision-support tools using appropriate techniques (e.g., mathematical programming, stochastic optimization, simulation, machine learning). Lead data acquisition, cleaning, integration, and analysis; establish data pipelines and quality assurance processes; conduct experiments, benchmarking, and sensitivity analyses to validate models. Prototype and, where applicable, support pilot deployment of solutions; document methodologies, assumptions, reproducible workflows, and user guides. Prepare high-quality publications, technical reports, and presentations; contribute to dissemination through conferences, seminars, and industry briefings. Guide and mentor PhD students, research assistants, and interns; provide technical supervision, review work outputs, and foster a collaborative research culture. Coordinate compliance with NUS and partner data governance, ethics, and security requirements; manage project documentation and IP considerations in line with agreements. Contribute to grant proposals, project scoping, and resource planning; assist with budgeting and procurement related to project needs. Support broader IORA initiatives, including knowledge sharing, seminar organization, and collaborations across the University and with external partners Requirements PhD in Industrial Engineering/Operations Research/Management Science (or closely related), with a strong research record in stochastic modeling and optimization. Hands-on experience solving real-world routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization problems. Proficiency with large-scale decision methods (e.g., approximate dynamic programming/rollout, primal–dual or penalty controls, Lagrangian decomposition/index policies, queueing/fluid models). Strong programming skills in Python and/or Java (MATLAB a plus), with experience in data pipelines, simulation/digital twins, and reproducible experimentation. Proven project management and industry collaboration experience, delivering milestones and translating operational needs into deployable solutions. Strong communication and mentorship: publications, technical reporting, presentations, and guidance of PhD students/RAs.

title: Senior (Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services) - Financial Services FAAS
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The opportunity Over the last 20 years, innovative new products have dramatically increased the complexity of the financial markets, from complex preference shares issuance in venture capital world, to structured products allowing clients to repackage and transfer risk, or other more exotic instruments designed to meet almost any goal two parties can agree on. We are looking to grow our Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services (“QATS”) team within the Financial Services - Financial Accounting Advisory Services (“FAAS”) service line. To be specific, we are looking for valuation and quant finance individuals to support Assurance teams with independent valuation and quantitative analysis on various financial instruments and investments into private enterprises. Included are opportunities to develop individual consulting skillsets and to provide services in model governance and valuation frameworks. The service we provide within EY covers a broad spectrum of responsibilities with respect to financial instruments and business valuation. You will experience ongoing professional development through your interactions with internal and external clients, in conjunction with exposure to various asset classes (e.g. FX, Fixed Income, Equity, Commodity, Interest Rate, Credit, etc.) and industry sectors (e.g. banking, private equity, hedge funds, transportation, commodity, utilities, oil & gas, REITS, e-commerce, fintech, etc). The QATS team at EY uses sophisticated techniques from the field of financial engineering and quantitative finance to assist clients in developing quantitative models for pricing, valuation, risk management and regulatory compliance purposes. Your key responsibilities: Financial Derivatives Valuation and Business Valuation Work together within a team on multiple client engagements in daily independent valuation for a diverse range of financial instruments Assist in developing valuation models to assess a variety of linear and non-linear financial instruments by adopting option pricing theory, fixed income and structured finance models, Monte Carlo simulation, statistical analysis, and other quantitative analysis Perform model validation on derivative pricing models, risk management models and model parameter calibration models Provide assistance in review of fair valuation approach, methodology, and assumptions adopted by private equity and venture capitalist clients Perform portfolio valuation review for private equity, venture capital, and asset management companies with investments Value debt instruments such as straight bonds, convertible bonds, redeemable convertible preference shares Build financial models, such as Option Pricing Model and Binomial Tree Model, to value hybrid instruments Credit Modelling Perform model development and model validations on credit risk models for Basel and IFRS 9 purposes Identify sources of model risk through comprehensive review of all model components and developmental evidence and challenging the assumptions and basis of which the components are being derived Employ statistical modelling methodologies in performing validation and produce results to be analyzed from statistical as well as business perspective. Keep updated on industry developments in terms of regulatory requirements and best practice modelling techniques Write high quality model validation reports and/or presentations Project Management Technically contribute to managing projects which includes packaging overall project findings into clear, concise and high-quality deliverables Ensure all modelling tasks are completed timely Data Analytics Design meaningful application of data analytics to be used for auditing financial numbers including developing toolkit for data mining and work paper documentation standards Use data analytics to provide actionable, timely and accurate data to drive business insights around customer, regulatory risk, compliance, operations and financial to achieve greater return on investment. General Maintain and develop positive, productive, and professional relationships with clients Build valuable relationships with external clients and internal peers Challenge yourself to continually learn and teach, mentoring others while developing your own career through various exposures in client projects Take ownership of your projects, while collaboratively working with other team members Skills and attributes for success Work effectively as a team member, sharing responsibility, providing support, maintaining communication and updating team members on progress Highly motivated, ability to multi-task, work independently under minimum supervision and deliver top-quality results in a fast-paced, dynamic environment Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills Strong written and verbal communication skills Advanced written and verbal communication skills Demonstrate integrity, values, principles, and work ethics within a professional environment Good project management skills The successful candidate must be able to display several achievements below. Within 6 months of joining, the candidate should Understand the mechanics and valuation methodology behind various derivative financial instruments and perform valuation for such financial instruments using EY developed tools Be able to explain the valuation differences between client’s and EY’s independent valuation through analyzing how various input parameters and model choices and assumptions drive the valuation output Be able to perform credit risk assessment, focusing on quantitative analysis based on financial standing, for both listed and non-listed companies Understand how various credit risk parameters are developed and validated Gain basic understanding on programming languages such as VBA and Numerix, and apply it on Lattice Model, Monte Carlo Simulation and other model implementations when working on valuation engagements and R, Python, SAS and VBA when working on credit modelling engagements. Be able to work independently with minimal supervision and take ownership of projects assigned to be under the candidate’s ownership Within 1 year of joining, the candidate should Confidently manage clients and resolve issues typically encountered by clients with regard to financial instrument and business valuations Understand the valuation approach and ability to explain the economics underpinning various types of interests and exposures in private equity and venture capitalist businesses (e.g. liquidation preferences of preference shares), and perform valuation for such financial instruments Understand the Hedge Accounting concept under Singapore Financial Reporting Standard (SFRS) framework and be able to perform the quantitative approach for the purpose of hedge effectiveness assessment Understand the Incremental Borrowing Rate concept under Singapore Financial Report Standard (SFRS) framework and be able to perform the quantitative approach for the purpose of Incremental Borrowing Rate assessment Be able to explain to general non-technical audiences on the basics of how various financial instruments function, and the benefits and risks that they bring Explain to general non-technical audiences on the rationale of the assumptions, methodologies and movements of various credit risk parameters Be adaptive and committed to an intense environment and tight deadlines in providing deliverables to stakeholders Possess excellent time management and prioritize various projects allocated to the candidate Be able to work together within the team and actively provide guidance to junior staff members in order to deliver high quality work products After 1 year, candidates who demonstrate strong competency in quantitative finance has the opportunity to join the Quantitative Development Team to develop pricing model templates To qualify for the role, you must have Bachelor's or master's degree in quantitative finance, financial engineering, finance, statistics, mathematical finance, or engineering with programming skills A good programming skill with demonstrated aptitude in quantitative analysis and understanding of at least one Finance software package (e.g. Numerix, Murex and Fincad), Bloomberg, Reuters, MS Excel/VBA Ideally, you’ll also have Prior working experience in the banking industry in valuation control, pricing/credit model development and validation, market risk function Prior working experience in global professional services organizations or mid-tier firms The Chartered Valuer and Appraiser designation or be on track to be a CVA holder all of which will be highly advantageous in your application What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent analytical skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. The successful candidate must also be committed to staying at the cutting edge of both the latest financial instruments valuation techniques, business developments and accounting standards. We're seeking leaders with the ability to quickly anticipate and respond to the issues of a networked and constantly changing global economy. What we offer Continuous learning: You’ll develop the mindset and skills to navigate whatever comes next. Success as defined by you: We’ll provide the tools and flexibility, so you can make a meaningful impact, your way. Transformative leadership: We’ll give you the insights, coaching and confidence to be the leader the world needs. Diverse and inclusive culture: You’ll be embraced for who you are and empowered to use your voice to help others find theirs.

title: SAP Master Data Lead
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Project Portfolio and Master Data Lead to join our Technical Team in Neste Tuas Singapore. In this job you will: Lead a team of  Document Integrity Controllers in managing Neste Digital Assets, including SAP Master Data, Engineering Document Depository and other Information Management System (IMS) applications. Function as the Project Portfolio controller to ensure data and information are accurate in SAP, and that approval processes and governance are consistently followed. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job: Master Data & Document Management Leadership : Providing leadership to Document Integrity Controllers in managing life-cycle documents and SAP Master Data. Accountable for SAP Master Data integrity and act as MDG Data Owner Accountable for Document Management system integrity and accessibility by end users. Facilitate data, documents and information handover from projects and MOCs initiatives. Drive continuous Improvement and clarity related to SAP MDG and document management systems, working closely with key stakeholders at site and globally. Provide comprehensive support to end-users, including training, troubleshooting issues and managing system administration tasks. 2. Project Portfolio Control and Support: Supporting Asset Development and Portfolio Manager in ensuring site project portfolio is updated in SAP (PPM and PS) Coordinate monthly site investment jury and portfolio review meeting. Support Development Manager/Engineer in setting up project, releasing WBS, and monitoring of the project budget, schedule and resources utilisation. Collaborate with cross site and global project portfolio team in implementing standardise improvement in portfolio system and processes. Liaise with finance team to ensure the accuracy of the project spending and forecast. Report creation and monitoring of relevant project portfolio information in Power BI Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Education: Diploma, Degree, or Master's in Engineering, Science, or Information Technology. Experience: 7–10 years of experience in the Process Manufacturing/Engineering industry, with a minimum of 2 years of experience in people management. Technical Expertise: Strong SAP skills, particularly in Project Portfolio Management (PPM), Project System (PS), Master Data Governance (MDG), and/or Information Management System (IMS). Knowledge and experience in Document Management Processes, IT, and Quality Systems. Knowledge of industry standards and regulations in this domain is an advantage. Good computer and business solutions software skills; experience with SAP and Google tools is considered an advantage. Soft Skills & Competencies: Quick learner, independent, with good analytical and problem-solving skills. Good Communication skills to different organizational levels. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application by 20 February 2026. We will do our best to contact you within 2 weeks after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The first interviews are preliminarily scheduled to take place soon. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Research Fellow (Aerospace Engineering/Transport/Mechanical Engineering/Computer Science)
description: Air Traffic Management Research Institute (ATMRI) is a research institute at Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore, supported by the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS). The key objective of ATMRI is to support research and innovation in air traffic management. ATMRI focuses on developing practical and data-driven solutions for complex aviation systems, in close collaboration with industry and international partners. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/atmri . We are looking for a Research Fellow to conduct research on air traffic management (ATM) algorithms and data-driven methods. The role will focus on developing intelligent methods and operation-support tools for ATM applications, contributing to the advancement of research and innovation at Nanyang Technological University. Key Responsibilities: Conduct research on methods and approaches to support air traffic management studies and applications. Develop, test, and evaluate analytical models, algorithms, and tools using operational and simulated data. Support research activities related to airspace and air traffic operations under current and future ATM concepts. Work collaboratively with researchers and engineers to develop prototype solutions and decision-support tools. Analyse results and assess the performance and applicability of developed methods in realistic operational scenarios. Prepare technical reports, research papers, and project deliverables, and participate in project meetings and reviews. Contribute to research proposals, technical documentation, and other project-related activities as required. Job Requirements: PhD qualification degree in Aerospace Engineering, Transport, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or other related fields. At least three (3) years of relevant research experience in air traffic management or related technical research roles. Familiarity with research related to air traffic management systems, airspace operations, or air traffic operations. Good written and oral communication skills with the ability to prepare technical reports and academic publications. Proficiency in job-specific technical skills, including programming for research and data analysis purposes (e.g. Python or similar languages), as well as strong numerical and analytical skills. Competent in general research-related skills and professional attributes, including problem-solving and analytical thinking. Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work independently and collaboratively, develop solutions under defined timelines, and demonstrate good organisational and time management skills. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Finance Manager
description: Finance and Business Planning is a highly mission driven organization - our mission is to enable Meta to plan, prioritize, and execute as effectively as possible. We take a uniquely long-term view towards business problems. It is critical that we operate with an independent and objective perspective and represent our point of view to our partners and executive stakeholders. We serve as financial advisers to our business partners and own shared business goals and results where appropriate. We strive to balance our responsibility to support business partners with our responsibility to serve the long-term mission and sustainability of Meta and act as stewards of company resources. Responsibilities Develop a quantitative and qualitative understanding of Meta's ads business model Create business partnerships with leaders of all levels and functions across product and data science teams Demonstrate the ability to create/socialize narratives and influence outcomes and decisions (not just report on analytics) Maintain/create core reporting and analytics infrastructure and models while proactively surfacing opportunities for improvement Drive elements of company-wide revenue forecast cycles: long-range plan (LRP), annual budget, and quarterly forecasts Improve processes and coordinate deliverables across many teams within Finance, as well as teams outside Finance Minimum qualifications 8+ years of experience in business strategy, finance, analytics, or planning experience BS, BA, or MS in Economics, Business, Math, Statistics, Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, or other quantitative field Experience with SQL and GSheets/Excel Preferred qualifications MBA or MS in quantitative field Experience in consulting, investment banking, investment management, or tech finance role Experience problem-solving individually and in a group setting Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

title: Cyber Security Analyst (Consultant)
description: Summary: The Cyber Security Analyst (CSA) is responsible for assisting with the day-to-day operations of securing the organization’s various information systems. Reporting to the Information Security Manager, the CSA is tasked with providing technical expertise in all areas of network, system, and application security. The CSA works closely with the various teams in the organization to ensure that systems and networks are always designed, developed, deployed, and managed with an emphasis on strong, effective security and risk management controls. The CSA leads the organization’s vulnerability management program, manages the annual cybersecurity assessments and penetration tests, and researches and reports on emerging threats, to help the organization take pre-emptive risk mitigation steps. The CSA effectively correlates and analyzes security events to proactively detect threats and mitigate attacks before they occur. Job Responsibilities: •	Proactively monitor the environment to detect and implement steps to mitigate cyber-attacks before they occur •	Provides technical expertise regarding security-related concepts to operational teams •	Review, investigate, and respond to real-time alerts within the environment •	Review real-time and historical reports for security and/or compliance violations •	Monitor online security-related resources for new and emerging cyber threats •	Assesses new security technologies to determine potential value for the enterprise •	Conducts vulnerability assessments of firm systems and networks •	Draft reports and presentations for clients. •	Keep abreast of the latest cybersecurity threats and technologies. •	Work with IT and other departments to ensure security best practices are followed Requirements •	A recognizes degree or equivalent industry training and certifications •	Three to five years of experience in a security analyst or related position •	Experience in developing security solutions on one or more cloud platform (AWS, GCP or Azure) and applying the cloud native security services •	Understanding of security concepts and tools - Certificates, DLP, WAF, SIEM, firewalls, DDOS prevention, •	IDS/IPS, privileged access management, Encryption, SSL, VPN, IPSec, TCP/IP, DNS and web security architecture etc •	Technical knowledge of enterprise-class technologies such as firewalls, routers, switches, wireless access points, VPNs, and desktop and server operating systems •	Thorough understanding of Microsoft’s enterprise technology platform, including Azure, Active Directory, •	SQL, Office365, and the Windows server and desktop operating systems •	Document risks, security controls and evidence to ensure compliance •	Strong writing skills, as well as the ability to articulate security-related concepts to a broad range of technical and non-technical staff •	Working experience with creating, implementing, and managing a threat hunting program within a corporate environment •	Demonstrated experience implementing and/or enforcing security and compliance frameworks •	Be a proficient problem-solver that is able to work autonomously Desired Qualifications: •	One or more of the following certifications: CEH, CISM, CompTIA Security+, CISSP, GSEC •	Experience with managing and securing both on-premise and hosted systems and applications •	Experience with application and database security •	Experience in setup of policies and procedures that ensure effective cloud resource management, monitoring, cost control, and operational efficiency

title: Cost Optimization Strategy Manager - Vice President
description: Are you ready to make a real impact on trading profitability? Join a team where your expertise drives both cost savings and revenue growth. You’ll collaborate with diverse colleagues, develop specialized skills, and gain a holistic view of trading profit and loss. Experience career mobility and the opportunity to influence business outcomes. Be part of a culture that values your ideas and supports your success. As a Cost Optimization Strategy Manager in the Markets Operations team, you will work within the Cost and Commission Services group to develop and execute strategies that optimize trading costs across Brokerage, Clearing, and Exchange for Asia Pacific markets. You will partner with trading desks across asset classes, including Equities, Interest Rates, Foreign Exchange, Credit, and Commodities, covering all major countries in the region. You will analyze trading data, collaborate with stakeholders, and help shape the future of cost management. You will be empowered to drive change, innovate, and make a measurable impact on the business. Our team specializes in optimizing trading costs and commissions, working closely with trading desks and business managers across Asia-Pacific. We support a wide range of asset classes and markets, leveraging advanced analytics and digital tools to deliver best-in-class solutions. You’ll have the opportunity to work on high-profile projects, contribute to business strategy, and grow your career in a dynamic, collaborative environment. Strong stakeholder management skills including navigating difficult situations, negotiating timelines, and influencing internal and external stakeholders across organizations and borders. An analytical, data-driven approach to decision-making – you understand how to partner with Data Science to build metrics and track progress across various monitoring programs. Excellent collaboration, communication, and relationship building skills. Must have the ability to convey complex ideas succinctly to all audiences. title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About The Job We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project “AI-Native Token Communication for Physical AI". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Physical AI. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of token communication and Physical AI over wireless networks. Develop distributed and scalable inference frameworks and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Co-supervise graduate students and research assistants. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in token communication, Physical AI, and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We’re Looking For A Doctoral degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with a specialization on wireless communication and distributed machine learning A strong background in communication theory, signal processing, and optimization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and MATLAB. A proven track record of publishing research papers in esteemed conferences or journals in wireless communication and AI. Strong communication skills and the capability to work effectively in a collaborative research environment.

title: Senior Audit Manager - T&O
description: JOB SUMMARY •    Group Internal Audit & Investigations (GIAI) in Standard Chartered Bank is looking for an exceptional and dynamic Senior Audit Manager to join our Financial Crime Surveillance (FCS) Operations team within the Technology & Operations portfolio. •    The role holder is expected to manage, lead and deliver the audit work covering key risks of FCS Operations and should have hands-on experience in auditing FCS Operations. •    Possess a deep understanding on digital assets, including but not limited to trading, processing and transaction monitoring of digital assets. •    Strong communicator, both written and verbal, with ability to clearly explain issues to Management and write audit issues, reports, and opinions. •    Ability to manage the team, provide coaching and mentoring to team members and lead auditors of a wide range of skills to deliver audits. •    Strong analytical skills with hands-on experience on Python, Power BI, Tableau, SQL. •    Proven skills in prompt engineering queries for artificial intelligence (AI), in the aid of audit planning, execution and delivery. RESPONSIBILITIES Audit Delivery •    Act as Team Manager or Team Leader to deliver audits timely. •    Execute audit work and issue validation in an efficient and effective manner, within the given budget and timelines. •    Provide clear guidance, detailed review, and supervision of the audit team’s work, so that audit deliverables meet quality standards. •    Clearly identify the risks and impact of issues during issue writing, agreeing these issues with management and obtaining quality management action plans to mitigate the risk. •    Provide guidance to the team on business/audit technical knowledge and management skills. •    Champion innovation and increase the use of leading edge methods, such as artificial intelligence and data analytics, in audit assignments. •    Ensure timely and open communication throughout the audit process, within GIAI and with the auditee. •    Track the implementation/delivery of the agreed issues and action plans for the audits assigned, understanding the key risks arising, provide advice on resolution of issues to auditees/action plan owners and escalate audit findings that remain unresolved. •    Carry out their role in line with the Audit Charter and remain independent from management and free from interference. Personal Behaviours •    Model valued behaviours and foster an environment that recognises positive contributions. •    Engage with peers to enhance knowledge sharing. •    Seek external perspectives to improve audit work. •    Provide expertise to support GIAI audit teams. •    Implement and support department initiatives, including planning and training. People & Talent •    Build and maintain relationships with key GIAI and FCS Operations stakeholders to understand business developments including regulatory concerns, identify emerging risks, and facilitate execution of audit work. •    Share knowledge, skills and experience with team members and the wider GIAI and provide advisory to internal auditors from both Business and Technology teams. •    Contribute to building a high performing collaborative function through regular feedback and coaching. Risk Management •    Complete relevant risk assessment activities in a timely and effective manner, including the completion of the Risk Assessment template. •    Assist in continuous auditing and monitoring of key risks in the portfolio. •    Follow changes in the laws and regulations to identify emerging risks. •    Utilise the risk assessment process and stakeholder engagement to determine an anticipatory audit plan. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Key Stakeholders •    Designated FCS Operations stakeholders, typically related to individual audit assignments and the assigned portfolio. •    GIAI stakeholders, including Team Members, Team Leaders, Team Managers, Accountable Executive Team, Portfolio, Functional, Country and Cluster Heads of Audit. Other Responsibilities •    Embed Here for Good and Group’s brand and values in GIAI. •    Perform other responsibilities assigned under Group, Country, Business or Functional policies and procedures. Our Ideal Candidate •    Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology or a related field •    10+ years of hands-on experience in the Audit, and/or Banking and Financial Services industry •    Chartered Accountant, Certified Internal Audit (CIA), Digital Assets related certification, Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist (CAMS) will be advantageous. Role Specific Technical Competencies •    Internal Audit •    Data Analytics (Python, Power BI, Tableau, SQL) •    Digital Assets •    Microsoft Word/Excel/PowerPoint About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

Conduct regular audits on data quality, qualification consistency, and process adherence. Provide structured feedback and training to teams to improve CRM discipline and customer experience. Implement controls to reduce risk linked to inaccurate data, improper follow-up, or non-compliant communication practices. Maintain documentation for workflows, qualification criteria, disqualification reasons, and system changes. Support audits and work with legal/privacy teams on risk mitigation and data-handling best practices. QUALIFICATIONS Essential Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Analytics, Information Systems, or related field. 5+ years of experience in CRM management, marketing operations, sales operations, or contact centre optimisation. Experience with automation tools, lead-scoring models, or customer lifecycle frameworks. Ability to create QA frameworks, SOPs, and training modules for CRM or contact centre teams. title: Technical Engineer
description: About ST Engineering ST Engineering is a global technology, defence, and engineering group with offices across Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and the U.S., serving customers in more than 100 countries. The Group uses technology and innovation to solve real-world problems and improve lives through its diverse portfolio of businesses across the aerospace, smart city, defence, and public security segments. Headquartered in Singapore, ST Engineering ranks among the largest companies listed on the Singapore Exchange. Our history spans more than 50 years, and our strategy is underpinned by our core values – Integrity, Value Creation, Courage, Commitment and Compassion. These 5 core values guide every aspect of our business and are embedded in our ST Engineering culture – from the people we hire, to working with each other, to our partners and customers. We support our client’s innovation journey through design thinking, analytics, and AI-enabled decision support with our full suite of cloud computing solutions. We provide intelligent, actionable insights and sustainable solutions to our valued partners in diverse industries including defence, government, and commercial sectors. Together, We Can Make A Significant Impact We are looking for an experienced and proactive Technical Engineer to support the technical development of our Core Platform. The role involves collaborating with vendors, customers, and internal teams to refactor, upgrade, and optimize the platform’s tech stack while also exploring new technologies and AI integrations. Experience with serverless architectures. Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and modern DevOps practices. Strong experience with microservices architecture and related technologies (REST APIs, messaging systems like Kafka). Experience in AI integration and working with AI/ML frameworks is a plus. Refactoring skills: Ability to refactor legacy code, improve code quality, and optimize performance. Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.. title: Senior Data Scientist
description: Synthesis x Senior Data Scientist At Synthesis, we push the boundaries of what’s possible with Open Data. It’s a fast-paced, rapidly-changing environment at the intersection of data science, cultural analysis & brand consultancy. We call this Open Source Intelligence for brands. As a Senior Data Scientist at Synthesis, you will play a key role in shaping our data science practice and driving innovation across both client projects and internal products. You will lead end-to-end data initiatives, delivering project work from shaping datasets and building models to designing scalable data workflows that power the insights and decision frameworks we deliver. You will work with diverse open data sources, ranging from search, social, and e-commerce, to academic papers and patents, to understand audience behaviour and anticipate how needs will evolve over time. In this role, you will not only contribute hands-on but also guide junior data scientists, act as a thought partner to cultural strategists, and help define the roadmap for our data products and infrastructure. We are a small, collaborative team with diverse backgrounds, spanning computer science and engineering, anthropology, and law. We value curiosity, creativity, and a spirit of experimentation. If you are excited about combining data and culture to solve real-world problems, we would love to hear from you! What you’ll need. 5+ years of experience in data science, with a strong record of delivering impactful projects from data exploration through to production deployment. Deep proficiency in Python and SQL, with strong software engineering fundamentals (clean, modular, well-documented code, testing, and version control with Git). Proven experience designing and building scalable data workflows or data-driven products, including architecting pipelines, modular components, and reusable frameworks. Experience applying large language models or advanced NLP techniques (e.g. embeddings, semantic parsing, topic modelling, text classification); familiarity with processing Asian languages is a plus. Strong grasp of statistical methods and core machine learning algorithms, with practical experience using libraries such as scikit-learn, statsmodels, and the ability to select appropriate models based on data characteristics and problem context. Comfortable working with large, messy, and unstructured datasets, particularly text and image-based data. Ability to translate complex technical insights into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders, and collaborate closely with strategists to drive business outcomes. A collaborative mindset, with openness to feedback and diverse perspectives. Bonus points if you have the following. Experience leading or mentoring junior data scientists, including setting technical direction and supporting professional growth. Experience building and maintaining production-grade data pipelines (e.g. Airflow, Dagster) or integrating data from APIs, web scraping, or third-party providers. Familiarity with cloud environments such as Google Cloud or AWS, and deploying data science workflows at scale Why Synthesis? Synthesis is known for delivering exceptional data models and products that uncover the stories of the people they represent and inspire our partners to act with confidence. Partnering with the world’s most successful brands in fashion, beauty, content, and travel, we’re building specialised solutions to solve problems of planning for the future. Rooted in culture, tested in data science, we spot and anticipate changes and connections in culture to inspire action and help brands grow with a new wave of audiences. After three years of iterative development and refinement, we are preparing to launch a series of products over the next 5 years. We will be at the forefront of a shift towards leveraging open data to develop rich, honest, human insight. In an industry that has, for too long, relied on a ‘question-response’ approach to understanding changes in behaviour, we prioritise layering behavioural, performative, search, and sales data to highlight discrepancies between what people say they do and what they actually do. We see a $40 billion industry that has failed to innovate for too long, and we are just getting started. About Synthesis We do Human Centred Data Science. It’s a way of reimaging open data sources from a human perspective. It prioritises behaviour and context to unravel why people do what they do, at scale.  By blending data science with human and market intelligence, we help our clients spot early signals of change and predict implications for business. Diverse by design. Synthesis is a team of digital researchers, gaming designers, data forecasters, network scientists, ethnographers, and engineers.  Cultural and category experts train our models – ensuring they detect measures that matter most – whilst data scientists uncover patterns the eye cannot see. Building bespoke, comprehensive datasets where none exist. We work with open data - search, ratings, social, and sales.  By layering many signals, we build robust and relevant data-driven stories around brands, businesses, and consumer behaviour. Asia first, global applied. Our products must work in Asia first - the hardest test markets.  We continue to make significant investments in language and capabilities across Asian-specific platforms.  Now applied in Europe, LatAm, and the USA.

description: Luxoft has one of the world's leading Orchestrade practices. We are a top-tier Orchestrade Partner and a market leader in implementation, integration, upgrade, and migration. Mandatory Skills Description: • Experience in a Hedge Fund organization is must • 10+ years of experience as a Business Analyst working on Financial products with strong understanding of listed and OTC products (equities, futures, options, fixed income, FX), order lifecycle, and risk/PnL concepts. • Excellent analytical skills, understanding of project life cycles, and ability to act in an entrepreneurial manner to ensure the requirements from the business are gathered in the appropriate manner. • Proven analytical, planning, problem solving and decision-making skills • Comfortable with direct communication with all levels of technical and business resources • Excellent organizational and time management skills with the ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously • Experience with working in a highly collaborative and dynamic multi-team environment • Experience in writing user stories and working with JIRA /similar tools. title: Project Manager - Market Risk
description: Project Description: Client is leading global financial institution with a strong presence in Asia, Americas, Europe, and the Middle East. In the Asia-Pacific region, we operate key hubs in Singapore and Hong Kong, providing comprehensive financial services across corporate banking, markets, treasury, and risk management. As part of this commitment, the Group invests in industry-leading systems such as Murex to support end-to-end trade processing, risk analytics, and regulatory compliance across multiple asset classes. title: Lead, Treasury Data & Analytics
description: JOB SUMMARY Business Lead – Delivery, IDL Analytics is responsible for end-to-end delivery and enhancement of intraday liquidity analytics solutions within the bank’s treasury and liquidity ecosystem. This role improves real-time visibility of liquidity positions and drives innovation in liquidity forecasting and analytics through data-driven tools and automation. The Treasury Data & Analytics SME role is a key role in the data squad servicing end to end Treasury functions. Key Responsibilities 1. Product & Delivery Ownership • Lead the design, development, and rollout of the bank’s Intraday Liquidity Analytics (IDL) analytics and related tools. • Translate business requirements from Treasury Markets, Liquidity team, and Treasury Services into technical and analytical solutions. • Oversee end-to-end project delivery, ensuring quality, scalability, and timely execution across multiple workstreams. • Partner with internal teams to integrate payment, settlement, and collateral data from RTGS, SWIFT, clearing systems, SAP LMS etc. 2. Analytics and Insights • Drive the creation of real-time dashboards and KPIs (e.g., peak usage, liquidity buffers, payment throughput). • Develop forecasting and scenario analytics to support stress testing, funding optimization, and Basel III reporting. • Collaborate with data scientists and quantitative teams to apply machine learning or predictive analytics for liquidity flow modelling. 3. Stakeholder Management • Act as the primary liaison between Treasury, CIB, WRB, Operations, Risk, and Technology teams. • Engage with senior management to communicate analytics insights, tool capabilities, and track project progress. • Manage vendor relationships and ensure alignment of external tools with IDL objectives. 4. Governance and Controls • Ensure all tools comply with regulatory standards and internal model governance frameworks. • Oversee data quality, lineage, and reconciliation across liquidity sources. 5. Business Methodology • To act as a business solution owner of the projects’ target state and support analysis included in relevant concept and methodology papers required for preparation of BRDs • To ensure that detailed requirements are documented in BRDs, and are duly signed off by relevant stakeholders • To ensure that the new solutions comply with internal procedures / external regulatory guidelines and project deliverables are properly understood by business stakeholders, project team, and end-users. 6. Team Leadership • Lead a cross-functional team of business analysts, and visualization specialists. • Promote an agile delivery culture, fostering collaboration, transparency, and continuous improvement. • Mentor team members in liquidity analytics, deliver best practices, and stakeholder engagement. Our Ideal Candidate •	The ideal candidate combines deep domain expertise in liquidity management with strong delivery management, data and analytics skills with strong exposure in AI. •	10+ years of experience in project or product delivery, ideally within a banking or fintech environment. • Technical and Analytical • o	Strong understanding of intraday liquidity concepts, Basel III monitoring tools, and treasury operations. o	Proficiency in data analytics tools (e.g., SAP LMS, Python, SQL, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik) and data platforms (e.g., Hadoop, Snowflake). o	Experience with financial messaging systems (SWIFT, TARGET2, Fedwire, CHAPS). o	Familiarity with core banking and treasury systems such as Murex, Calypso, Finastra, or proprietary platforms. o	Experience in BI applications and related change management • Delivery and Leadership o	Proven track record managing complex data and regulatory reporting projects. o	Strong understanding of Agile methodologies and DevOps frameworks. o	Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills at executive and regulatory levels. Preferred Qualifications • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Finance, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field. • Professional certifications such as PMP, PRINCE2, CFA, or FRM. • Experience in liquidity risk analytics, intraday funding, or payment system modernization programs. • Exposure to AI/ML use cases in liquidity forecasting and ISO 20022 payment data transformation. Role Specific Technical Competencies • Change / Programme Management • Agile methodology / strategic planning • Data Analysis and story-telling • Stakeholder Management and Influence • Effective Communications • Knowledge of Organizational Design • Trading and Treasury products • Understanding of Bank systems architecture About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Cybersecurity Project Manager

duties, least privilege Networking Fundamentals Operating System Fundamentals Identity & Access Management Cryptographics Basics Application Security Fundamentals, such as Common vulnerabilities, As "Architects of Positive Futures", Capgemini actively supports the community in 3 ways: Diversity and Inclusion - we believe diversity of thought fuels excellence and innovation, which is why we positively encourage applications from suitably qualified candidates regardless of their gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religion, ability, intersex status or age.
